coverage_profile.seniority_signal: Partner, Chief Investment Officer, and Director of Research — title indicates senior, portfolio-level role; management addresses him informally as "Ivan," suggesting recurring access and respect (2022-Q1).
coverage_profile.sector_lens: Cruise / leisure / consumer travel with a focus on guest experience, digital engagement, and ancillary revenue (questions in 2022-Q1 center on pre-cruise app and incremental revenue).
rhetorical_signature: Opener: polite, collegial opening — "Hi. Thanks for taking my question" (2022-Q1).
Uses concrete hypotheticals and "let's say" examples to probe implementation (e.g., "Let's say if there was downtime in the spa").
Closes succinctly and thanks management repeatedly — short sign-offs such as "All right. Sounds appreciated. Thanks."

reasoning_style.justification: Questions combine qualitative strategy (app functionality, marketing cadence) with a demand for commercial impact ("add incremental revenue specifically outside of just onboard spending") — see 2022-Q1.
reasoning_style.follow_up_pattern: Multi-part initial questions followed by multiple short follow-ups/clarifications; ≈ 33% of turns contain ≥ 2 explicit question clauses (2 of 6 turns show multi-clause follow-ups) and he pushes for clarification rather than adversarial pushback.
reasoning_style.evidence_demanded: He rewards mechanisms and incremental commercial metrics — asks how the app "adds incremental revenue specifically outside of just onboard spending" (2022-Q1). He does not ask for bps; count of turns using 'bps' = 0.
reasoning_style.anchoring_habits: Anchors to management product/initiative names and management statements delivered on the call (e.g., reacts to the management framing that "pre-cruise revenue is fundamentally about onboard spend" and to "Project Excalibur" in 2022-Q1). There is no history of anchoring to peers in this sample (n_calls = 1).

core_topics.what_they_press_on: What systems the pre-cruise planning app connects to and the concrete user flows (booking, calendar, reminders) that enable a seamless guest experience.
How the app is used to proactively market before and onboard and whether that drives incremental revenue beyond onboard spending.
Operational use-cases such as booking spa appointments and re-routing demand (e.g., "if there was downtime in the spa, you could connect with somebody").

recurring_concerns.blind_spots: n_calls < 2; insufficient to characterise many recurring blind spots across quarters. Within the 2022-Q1 record, he does not press on fuel hedging, fleet capacity/itinerary cadence, guidance/yield figures, or balance-sheet/liquidity metrics — 0 turns on these topics in this sample.
recurring_concerns.stance_drift: n_calls < 2; insufficient to characterise stance drift across quarters. Only 2022-Q1 available, so no multi-quarter comparison is possible.

queue_behavior.typical_position: #10–#11 on the single observed call (2022-Q1); he was among the final questioners/"last question" on that call.
queue_behavior.default_first_pick: No evidence in history — he was not observed as an early/first questioner in the provided calls (only 2022-Q1, where he was late).

evidence.quote: add incremental revenue specifically outside of just onboard spending
proactively market both before and/or use it to proactively market

explicit_peer_references.examples: no explicit peer-citation phrases in the provided turns

examples.quote: pre-cruise revenue is fundamentally about onboard spend

management_line_anchoring.note: He explicitly pushes against that management framing by asking for incremental revenue "outside of just onboard spending."

cross_analyst_reactivity.operator_or_followup_signal: Took multiple follow-up utterances within the same call (4 follow-up utterances beyond the initial substantive question in 2022-Q1) primarily for clarification and deeper drill into functionality and monetization rather than to introduce a new topic.
